questions.prompt:
Quantity A: 39 − (25 − 17)
Quantity B: 39 − 25 − 17
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Simplify Quantity A: 39 − (25 − 17) = 39 − 8 = 31. Quantity B: 39 − 25 − 17 = −3. Quantity A is greater.

questions.prompt:
Quantity A: 14 − 3(4 − 6)
Quantity B: (4)(−3)(2)(−1)
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Compute inside parentheses first: 14 − 3(4 − 6) = 14 − 3(−2) = 14 + 6 = 20. Quantity B: 4·(−3)·2·(−1) = 24. Quantity B is greater.

questions.prompt:
Quantity A: −5 × 1 ÷ 5
Quantity B: −6 × 1 ÷ 6
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Quantity A: −5 × 1 ÷ 5 = −5 ÷ 5 = −1. Quantity B: −6 × 1 ÷ 6 = −6 ÷ 6 = −1. The quantities are equal.

questions.prompt: What is the value of 5 − (4 − (3 − (2 − 1))) ?
questions.explanation: Work from the innermost parentheses: (2−1)=1 → (3−1)=2 → (4−2)=2 → 5−2=3.

questions.prompt:
Quantity A: −(2^3)/2
Quantity B: (−2)^2
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Quantity A: 2^3=8, so −8/2=−4. Quantity B: (−2)^2=4. Quantity B is greater.

questions.prompt:
Quantity A: 5^3 − 5^2
Quantity B: 5
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Compute: 5^3−5^2 = 125−25 = 100. Quantity A is greater.

questions.prompt:
Quantity A: −10 − (−3)^2
Quantity B: −[10 + (−3)^2]
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Quantity A: −10 − 9 = −19. Quantity B: −(10+9)=−19. The quantities are equal.

questions.prompt:
Quantity A: (30,000,000)(2,000,000)
Quantity B: (15,000,000)(4,000,000)
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: 30 million × 2 million = 60 million-million. 15 million × 4 million = 60 million-million. The quantities are equal.
In each row, the negative terms cancel the matching positives (e.g., −2+2 and −1+1). Only the last two columns contribute. Sum those: (3+4)+(6+8)+(9+12)+(12+16)+(15+20)+(18+24)=147.

questions.prompt:
Molly worked at an amusement park over the summer. Every two weeks, she was paid according to the following schedule:
• At the end of the first 2 weeks, she received $160.
• At the end of each subsequent 2-week period, she received $1 plus an additional amount equal to the sum of all payments she had received in previous weeks.
How much money was Molly paid during the full 10 weeks of summer?

A book with 80,000 words costs $24 and a short story with 1,000 words costs $1.
Quantity A: Cost per word of the book
Quantity B: Cost per word of the short story
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Book: 24/80,000 = 0.0003 dollars/word. Short story: 1/1,000 = 0.001 dollars/word. Quantity B is greater.

questions.prompt:
Ticket Prices at the Natural History Museum:
• Child (ages 5–18): Weekdays $7, Weekends & Holidays $9
• Adult (ages 19–64): Weekdays $14, Weekends & Holidays $16
• Senior (ages 65+): Weekdays $8, Weekends & Holidays $10
• Children under age 5 attend free

Quantity A: The price for tickets at the Natural History Museum on a weekday for one 12-year-old and one 39-year-old
Quantity B: The price for tickets at the Natural History Museum on a weekend for one 4-year-old, two 8-year-olds, and one senior over 65 years old, after applying a coupon for $10 off the total cost
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Quantity A: 7 + 14 = 21. Quantity B: (0 + 9 + 9 + 10) − 10 = 18. Quantity A is greater.

questions.prompt:
On a certain train, tickets cost $6 each for children and $9 each for adults. The total train ticket cost for a certain group of six passengers was between $44 and $50.
Quantity A: The number of children in the group
Quantity B: The number of adults in the group
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Possible totals: 3 adults + 3 children → 3·9 + 3·6 = 45 (A=B). 4 adults + 2 children → 4·9 + 2·6 = 48 (B>A). More than one relationship is possible, so it cannot be determined.

questions.prompt: If 617 is divided by 49, the sum of the tens digit and the tenths digit of the resulting number is what value?
questions.explanation: 617 ÷ 49 = 12.5918… Tens digit is 1; tenths digit is 5; sum = 6.

questions.prompt:
Quantity A: The number of days between May 30, 1917, and May 15, 1996, inclusive
Quantity B: The number of days between May 15, 1912, and May 30, 1991, inclusive
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Both ranges span about 79 years, but Quantity B starts earlier in May and ends later in May than Quantity A, so Quantity B includes more days.

questions.prompt:
Alfred’s Coffee Shop offers a “buy six cups of coffee, get one free” discount, and Boris’s Coffee Shop offers 15% off all orders of six or more cups of coffee. At both shops, the regular price of a single cup of coffee is $2.60.
Quantity A: The total cost for one order of seven single cups of coffee from Alfred’s
Quantity B: The total cost for one order of seven single cups of coffee from Boris’s
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Alfred’s: pay for 6 cups → 6·2.60 = 15.60. Boris’s: 7·2.60·0.85 = 15.47. Quantity A is greater.

questions.prompt: In a certain ancient kingdom, the standard unit of measure was the “crown,” equal to 10 standard modern inches. An alternative unit of measure was the “scepter,” equal to 14 standard modern inches. If a tower measured 70 crowns tall, how many scepters tall was it?
questions.explanation: 70 crowns = 70·10 = 700 inches. 700 ÷ 14 = 50 scepters.

questions.prompt: A total of $450 was donated to charity by 25 employees. If 15 employees donated at least $12 but less than $19 and 9 employees donated at least $19, what is the maximum amount, in dollars, that the last employee could have donated?
questions.explanation: Maximize the last donation by minimizing the others: 15·12 + 9·19 = 351. Remaining = 450 − 351 = 99.

questions.prompt: A tank has a capacity of 200 pints. How many gallons of water would it take to fill the tank to 3/10 of its capacity? (1 gallon = 8 pints)
questions.explanation: 3/10 of 200 pints = 60 pints. Convert to gallons: 60 ÷ 8 = 7.5 gallons.

questions.prompt:
1 kilogram = 2.2 pounds
Quantity A: The number of kilograms in 44 pounds
Quantity B: The number of pounds in 44 kilograms
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Quantity A divides by 2.2; Quantity B multiplies by 2.2, so Quantity B is greater.

questions.prompt: If the formula for converting degrees Fahrenheit to degrees Celsius is C = (5/9)(F − 32), what is the value of F when C is 30?
On a trip, Joe’s car traveled an average of 36 miles per gallon of fuel. Approximately how many kilometers did the car travel on 10 liters of fuel? (5 miles ≈ 8 kilometers; 1 gallon ≈ 4 liters)
questions.explanation: 36 mi/gal × (8 km/5 mi) = 57.6 km/gal. Per liter: 57.6/4 = 14.4 km/L. For 10 L: 10×14.4 = 144 km.

questions.prompt: How many 1-inch square tiles would it take to cover the floor of a closet that has dimensions 5 feet by 4 feet? (1 foot = 12 inches)
questions.explanation: Convert to inches first: 5 ft = 60 in, 4 ft = 48 in. Area = 60×48 = 2,880 square inches, so 2,880 tiles.

questions.prompt:
Child A ate 3/5 of a kilogram of chocolate and Child B ate 300 grams of chocolate. (1 kilogram = 1,000 grams)
Quantity A: The weight, in grams, of the chocolate that Child A ate
Quantity B: Twice the weight, in grams, of the chocolate that Child B ate
Compare the two quantities.
choices: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given
questions.explanation: Child A: (3/5)·1000 = 600 g. Quantity B: 2·300 = 600 g. The quantities are equal.

questions.prompt: Out of 5.5 billion bacteria grown for an experiment, 1 in 75 million has a particular mutation. Approximately how many of the bacteria have the mutation?
A particular nation’s GDP (Gross Domestic Product) is $4.5 billion. If the population of the nation is 1.75 million, what is the per capita (per person) GDP, rounded to the nearest dollar?
Global GDP (Gross Domestic Product) was $69.97 trillion in 2011. If the world population for 2011 was best estimated at 6,973,738,433, approximately what was the global GDP per person?

The runners on a cross country team ran a 5-mile race at average (arithmetic mean) speeds ranging from 4 miles per hour to 7 miles per hour, inclusive. Which of the following are possible race completion times for individual members of the team? Indicate all such times.
questions.explanation: Time = distance ÷ speed. With speeds 4 to 7 mph, times range from 5/7 hr ≈ 42.86 min (fastest) to 5/4 hr = 75 min (slowest). Valid choices are those between 42.86 and 75 minutes inclusive: 48, 60, and 75 minutes.
